code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a politician into a readable sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Template mapping for all predicates
    templates = {
        "birthPlace": "born in {obj}",
        "birthDate": "born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathYear": "died in {obj}",
        "nationality": "{obj} national",
        "party": "member of the {obj} party",
        "office": "held the office of {obj}",
        "successor": "succeeded by {obj}",
        "predecessor": "preceded by {obj}",
        "spouse": "married to {obj}",
        "residence": "resides at {obj}",
        "region": "from the region of {obj}",
        "country": "from {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "largest city is {obj}",
        "currency": "uses {obj} currency",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "leader": "led by {obj}",
        "battle": "participated in {obj}",
        "almaMater": "educated at {obj}",
        "profession": "worked as a {obj}",
        "religion": "follows {obj} faith",
        "award": "received {obj} award",
        "activeYearsStartDate": "active from {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndDate": "active until {obj}",
        "militaryBranch": "served in {obj}",
        "militaryRank": "held rank of {obj}",
        "serviceStartYear": "began service in {obj}",
        "state": "from {obj}",
        "governingBody": "governed by {obj}",
        "affiliation": "affiliated with {obj}",
        "inOfficeWhileVicePresident": "served while vice president was {obj}",
        "inOfficeWhilePrimeMinister": "served while prime minister was {obj}",
        "inOfficeWhilePresident": "served while president was {obj}",
        "inOfficeWhileGovernor": "served while governor was {obj}",
        "inOfficeWhileMonarch": "served while monarch was {obj}",
        "hasDeputy": "had {obj} as deputy",
        "unit": "served in {obj}",
        "commander": "commanded by {obj}",
        "isPartOfMilitaryConflict": "part of {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "ethnic group includes {obj}",
        "garrison": "garrisoned at {obj}",
        "place": "took place in {obj}",
        "trainerAircraft": "used {obj} trainer aircraft",
        "colour": "party colour is {obj}",
        "numberOfVotesAttained": "received {obj} votes",
        "default": "{obj}"
    }

    # Build a single complex sentence
    main_subject = list(subject_triples.keys())[0]
    t_list = subject_triples[main_subject]

    # Sort predicates by importance for natural flow (using dict for efficiency)
    priority_order = ["birthPlace", "birthDate", "birthYear", "nationality", "party", "office", "almaMater", "profession", "deathPlace", "deathDate", "deathYear"]
    priority_dict = {pred: i for i, pred in enumerate(priority_order)}
    sorted_triples = sorted(t_list, key=lambda t: priority_dict.get(t.predicate, len(priority_order)))

    clauses = []
    for i, t in enumerate(sorted_triples):
        template = templates.get(t.predicate, templates["default"])
        clause = template.format(obj=t.object)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Build complex sentence with proper connectors
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}."
    elif len(clauses) == 2:
        return f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]} and {clauses[1]}."
    else:
        # Join with commas, use 'and' before last clause
        return f"{main_subject} {', '.join(clauses[:-1])}, and {clauses[-1]}."

    # Handle multiple subjects with relationships
    if len(subject_triples) > 1:
        all_clauses = []
        for subject, t_list in subject_triples.items():
            for t in t_list:
                template = templates.get(t.predicate, templates["default"])
                clause = template.format(obj=t.object)
                all_clauses.append(f"{subject} {clause}")

        if len(all_clauses) > 1:
            return f"{all_clauses[0]}, and {'; '.join(all_clauses[1:])}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
